# Steward

> Steward is an open-source, control-plane-neutral sovereign admission and Linux
> execution runtime for AI agents. It combines signed local policy, tenant-bound
> instance intent, Docker/gVisor enforcement, rollback fences, and offline-verifiable
> node receipts without depending on a particular control plane.

Version documented: v1.2.0
License: Apache-2.0
Source: https://github.com/hardrails/steward
Documentation: https://hardrails.github.io/steward/

## Start here

- [Overview](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/): Product purpose, boundary, and documentation map
- [Install](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/getting-started/): Interactive and unattended Linux installation
- [Why Steward exists](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/product/positioning/): Product thesis and proof boundary
- [Market analysis](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/product/market-analysis/): Dated competitive matrix and research signals
- [Limitations](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/limitations/): Exact v1.2 capability boundary
- [FAQ](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/faq/): Common product and operational questions

## How-to guides

- [Workload lifecycle](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/workload-lifecycle/): Use the host-local Executor API
- [Signed admission](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/signed-admission/): Sign capsules and policy, configure fences and receipts, verify offline
- [Hermes Agent](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/hermes-agent/): v1.2 image/lifecycle compatibility procedure
- [OpenClaw](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/openclaw/): v1.2 image/lifecycle compatibility procedure
- [Air-gapped installation](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/air-gapped/): Offline import and verification
- [Upgrade and rollback](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/guides/upgrades/): Atomic release activation

## Explanation

- [Architecture](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/architecture/): Process and system boundaries
- [Security model](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/security-model/): Trust, isolation, and residual risks
- [Control-plane boundary](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/concepts/control-plane-boundary/): Steward versus an independent control plane

## Reference

- [Platform support](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/platform-support/): Supported hosts and artifacts
- [Configuration](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/configuration/): Flags, defaults, paths, and validation
- [APIs](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/api/): Authoritative OpenAPI contracts
- [Release artifacts](https://hardrails.github.io/steward/reference/release-artifacts/): Filenames and checksum verification

## Important v1.2 fact

Steward v1.2 can cryptographically admit and lifecycle-test Hermes Agent and
OpenClaw images but cannot yet run either as a connected persistent service.
Signed state, inference, and service ceilings exist in the artifact contract, but
Executor intentionally returns HTTP 501 until their isolated enforcement paths ship.
